subject: Product Management
title: Gmail
description: A short lesson to help you apply Gmail.

hook.text: Google launched Gmail on April 1, 2004. Everyone thought it was an April Fools' joke. 1 GB of storage? Impossible.

buildup.text: Hotmail offered 2 MB. Yahoo offered 4 MB. Gmail offered 1,000 MB — 250 times more than the leading competitor.

discovery.text: People delete email because of storage limits, not desire. Give them space and they keep everything.

twist.text: Gmail was invite-only for three years. Scarcity created demand — Gmail invites sold on eBay for $150 each.

climax.text: Gmail redefined email by treating it as searchable archive instead of a mailbox you had to constantly empty.

punchline.text: Give people what others say is impossible. Watch them flock.

quiz.question: What product insight drove Gmail's success?
options: People delete email because of storage limits — abundant storage changes behavior
Users wanted to pay for premium storage
